---
title: "Wilson N. Merrell"
page-layout: full
about:
  template: trestles
  image: images/portrait.jpg
  image-alt: "Wilson N. Merrell"
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:wmerrell@ps.au.dk
    - text: "CV"
      href: "https://docs.google.com/document/d/1D-XhHOa9tuIj76mlPQodSLwbSvlDAktx/edit?usp=drive_link&ouid=107821746053919011512&rtpof=true&sd=true"
      icon: file-text
    - text: "Google Scholar"
      href: "https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=fwDh3l4AAAAJ&hl=en"
      icon: mortarboard-fill
---

## Welcome!

I'm Wilson, a postdoc within the [Center for the Experimental-Philosophical Study of Discrimination (CEPDISC)](https://cepdisc.au.dk/) at Aarhus University (Denmark) and guest researcher in the Department of Psychology at the University of Oslo (Norway).

I received my PhD in social psychology from the University of Michigan in 2024.

I study how people navigate social threats (like avoiding illness) and opportunities (like seeking status) in ways that shape interpersonal behavior and intergroup processes.

My work focuses on two core puzzles: how people manage the tension between disease avoidance and social connection, and how they navigate group boundaries in contexts of status and hierarchy.

I like to use a variety of methods in my work, including experiments, geospatial modeling, and visual reverse correlation, oftentimes drawing on theory from evolutionary biology, political science, and anthropology.

See the [Research](research.qmd) tab for publications and [Teaching & Mentoring](teaching-mentoring.qmd) for educational experiences.

I have also been fortunate to teach and mentor many students in both classroom and lab settings. My pedagogical interests include equity-focused research methods teaching, debunking psychology myths, and examining evolutionary psychology in undergraduate curricula.

Prior to my postdoctoral position and graduate studies, I received a BA in Economics and Psychology from Macalester College (Saint Paul, MN) and worked as a research analyst at The Brattle Group (Washington, DC). I grew up in Poughkeepsie, NY, a place primarily know for one of the longest walking bridges in the world, Vassar College, and an episode of *Friends*.

Contact: wmerrell@ps.au.dk
